name: HRA Calculator
description: Estimate HRA exemption based on rent and salary.
formula: Exemption = min(HRA received, rent - 10% salary, 50% salary)

example.explanation: HRA exemption is the minimum of HRA received (₹30,000), rent minus 10% salary (₹15,000), and 50% salary (₹40,000).

faqs.question: What's my effective tax rate?
faqs.answer: Total tax paid ÷ Total income × 100. Progressive taxation means higher earners pay higher rates.

faqs.question: What deductions can I claim?
faqs.answer: 80C (₹1.5L): LI, PPF, NPS. 80D (health): ₹25-50K. 80E (education): ₹1.5L. HRA, LTA as applicable.

faqs.question: Is cryptogain taxed?
faqs.answer: Yes, as capital gains. Short-term (< 2 years): slab rate. Long-term (> 2 years): 20% with indexation benefit.

faqs.question: What happens if I file taxes late?
faqs.answer: Late filing fee: ₹1,000-5,000. Miss ITR deadline for capital loss carry-forward (allowed 8 years).

faqs.question: Can I reduce my tax liability?
faqs.answer: Yes, via 80C investments (PPF, LIC, NPS). Max benefit ₹1.5L investment saves ~₹45K tax (30% slab).

faqs.question: What's the difference between taxable and take-home?
faqs.answer: Taxable income = salary - deductions. Take-home = taxable income - income tax paid.

concept: Tax calculators help you understand your tax liability, savings opportunities, and effective tax rate under current Indian tax laws.
guideSteps: Enter your total annual income from all sources.
Add details of deductions (80C, 80D, 80E, HRA, etc.).
Input details of long-term and short-term capital gains.
Click Calculate to see your taxable income and tax liability.
Explore tax-saving strategies using Section 80C investments.
standards: Individuals have basic exemption: ₹2.5-3.5 lakhs depending on age.
Tax slabs: 0%, 5%, 20%, 30% for different income brackets.
Section 80C limit: ₹1.5 lakhs for investments in LI, PPF, NPS, etc.
HRA exemption: Lower of rent, 50% salary, or ₹15,000/month.
Long-term capital gains: 20% (+ cess) with indexation benefit.
